title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. radicans
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. radicans, commonly known as poison ivy, is a plant species native to eastern North America. The plant contains urushiol, an oily sap that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in many people. In addition to its toxic properties, poison ivy has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of poison ivy, such as tinctures and salves, have been used to treat conditions like eczema and acne, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation.
